meta.release_notes:
## Changelog

All notable changes to this model will be documented in this file.

## [4.0.0] 25.12

### Changed

- introduced `io.catenax.shared.industry_core.common:1.0.0` and `io.catenax.shared.quantity:3.0.0` as a shared model
- renamed `catenaXId` to `globalAssetId`

## [2.0.0] - 2024-02-26

### Added

- added new `customers` property as child of the `SingleLevelUsageAsPlanned` aspect property including its `ListOfCustomersCharactersistics` and `Customer` entity
- added new `businessPartner` property with its `BpnlTrait` of the shared business_partner_number 2.0.0 aspect model
- added new links from the `customers` entity to the `businesspartner`, `createdOn` and `lastModifiedOn` properties

### Changed

- changed `parentCatenaXId` to `catenaXId` to be conform with the data chain template
- renamed `parentParts` property to be `parentItems`
- changed `parentItems` property to be a mandatory child of the `SingleLevelUsageBuilt` aspect property instead of a child of the `customers` property
- replaced the `quantityNumber` and `measurementUnit` properties with the `itemUnit` and `quantityValue` properties of the shared quantity 2.0.0 aspect model
- replaced the existing `Timestamp` characteristics of `validFrom` and `validTo` with a new `DateTimeTrait`, to permit date information excluding time
- replaced the whole `CatenaXIdTraitCharacteristi`c and its childs with the `UuidV4Trait` of the shared uuid 2.0.0 aspect model
- description of some objects were adjusted

## [1.1.0]

### Added

- optional validity period for child-parent relation

### Changed

- descriptions to more explicitly describe handling of versions of child parts
- updated reference for SAMM Unit Catalog to a more stable one
- all characteristics, entities, and constraints now have proper names, preferred names and descriptions
- fixed some typos in preferred names and descriptions

### Removed

## [1.0.0] - 2022-12-07

### Added

- initial model

### Changed

n/a

### Removed

nodes.label: SingleLevelUsageAsPlanned
nodes.description:
The aspect provides the information in which parent part(s)/product(s) the given item is assembled in. This could be a 1:1 relationship in terms of a e.g. a brake component or 1:n for e.g. coatings. The given item as well as the parent item must refer to an object from as-planned lifecycle phase.
If multiple versions of parent parts exist that the child part can be assembled into, all versions of the parent part are included in the usage list.

nodes.label: catenaXId
nodes.description: The Catena-X ID of the given part (e.g. the assembly), valid for the Catena-X dataspace.

nodes.label: parentParts
nodes.description: Set of parent parts, in which the given child object is assembled in (one structural level up).

nodes.label: parentCatenaXId
nodes.description: The Catena-X ID of the parent object, into which the given child object is assembled in.

nodes.label: createdOn
nodes.description: Timestamp when the relation between the parent part and the child part was created.

nodes.label: quantity
nodes.description: Quantity of which the child part is assembled into the parent part.

nodes.label: lastModifiedOn
nodes.description: Timestamp when the assembly relationship between parent part and child part was last modified.

nodes.label: validityPeriod
nodes.description:
The period of time during which the parent-child relation is valid. This relates to whether a child part can be built into the parent part at a given time.
If no validity period is given the relation is considered valid at any point in time.

nodes.label: quantityNumber
nodes.description: The number of objects related to the measurement unit

nodes.label: measurementUnit
nodes.description:
Unit of measurement for the quantity of objects.
If possible, use units from the aspect meta model unit catalog, which is based on the UNECE Recommendation No. 20 "Codes for Units of Measure used in International Trade"

nodes.label: validFrom
nodes.description: Start date of validity period

nodes.label: validTo
nodes.description: End date of validity period

nodes.label: CatenaXIdTraitCharacteristic
nodes.description: Trait to ensure UUID v4 data format

nodes.label: SetOfParentPartsCharacteristic
nodes.description: Set of parent parts the given child object is assembled in (one structural level up).

nodes.label: Uuidv4Characteristic
nodes.description: A version 4 UUID is a universally unique identifier that is generated using random 32 hexadecimal characters.

nodes.label: ValidityPeriodCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic  for a validity period defined by an (optional) start and an (optional) end timestamp.

nodes.label: NumberOfObjects
nodes.description: Quantifiable number of objects in reference to the measurementUnit

nodes.label: Uuidv4RegularExpression
nodes.description: The provided regular expression ensures that the UUID is composed of five groups of characters separated by hyphens, in the form 8-4-4-4-12 for a total of 36 characters (32 hexadecimal characters and 4 hyphens), optionally prefixed by "urn:uuid:" to make it an IRI.

nodes.label: ParentData
nodes.description: Catena-X ID and meta data of the parent part.

nodes.label: Quantity
nodes.description: Comprises the number of objects and the unit of measurement for the respective child objects

nodes.label: ValidityPeriodEntity
nodes.description: If a validity period only has a start that means that the period is valid from the start date without a (yet) defined enddate and vice versa.

nodes.label: QuantityCharacteristic
nodes.description: Describes the quantity in which the child part is assembled in the parent object by providing a quantity value and the measurement unit in which the quantity is measured.
